NCT03823287.name: TENAYA
NCT03823287.group: Treatment-naive nAMD, faricimab 6 mg up-to-Q16W vs aflibercept 2 mg Q8W (wk 48, BCVA change)

allOutcomes.title: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (primary, faricimab minus aflibercept MD)
Faricimab patients achieving Q12W or longer dosing interval at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Faricimab patients achieving Q16W dosing interval at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Central subfield thickness change at week 48 (faricimab minus aflibercept; um, MD; secondary)

NCT03823287.snippet: Source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2022;399:729-740 (TENAYA).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,329 adults with treatment-naive neovascular age-related macular degeneration randomized 1:1 double-blind to faricimab 6 mg intravitreal up-to-every-16-weeks (personalised treatment interval after 4 initial Q4W doses; n=334) or aflibercept 2 mg every 8 weeks fixed (n=337) for 48 weeks. TENAYA was one of two pivotal replicate trials.

evidence.label: Primary - BCVA Change at Week 48
evidence.text: Adjusted mean change in best-corrected visual acuity letter score from baseline at weeks 40, 44, 48: faricimab +5.8 letters vs aflibercept +5.1 letters; estimated treatment difference +0.7 letters (95% CI -0.7 to +2.1). Non-inferiority margin of -4 letters met. Primary endpoint met (non-inferiority).

evidence.label: Secondary - Dosing Interval and Anatomic Outcomes
evidence.text: 80% of TENAYA faricimab patients achieved Q12W or longer dosing interval by wk 48; 45% achieved Q16W. Central subfield thickness reduction comparable to aflibercept.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind (masked investigator assessment of BCVA; sham injections where appropriate). D3 LOW - 94% completion. D4 LOW - BCVA measured by certified examiners using ETDRS chart. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Treatment-naïve choroidal neovascularization (CNV) secondary to age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) in the study eye~* Ability to comply with the study protocol, in the investigator's judgment~* For women of childbearing potential: agreement to remain abstinent (refrain from heterosexual intercourse) or use acceptable contraceptive measures that result in failure rate <1% per year during the treatment period and for at least 3 months after the final dose of study trea...

NCT03823300.name: LUCERNE
NCT03823300.group: Treatment-naive nAMD, faricimab up-to-Q16W vs aflibercept Q8W (wk 48, BCVA change)

allOutcomes.title: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (primary, faricimab minus aflibercept MD)
Faricimab patients achieving Q12W or longer dosing interval at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Faricimab patients achieving Q16W dosing interval at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Central subfield thickness change at week 48 (faricimab minus aflibercept; um, MD; secondary)

NCT03823300.snippet: Source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2022;399:729-740 (LUCERNE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,327 adults with treatment-naive neovascular AMD randomized 1:1 double-blind to faricimab up-to-Q16W (n=331) or aflibercept Q8W (n=327) for 48 weeks. LUCERNE was the replicate pivotal trial to TENAYA; pooled analyses based on matched protocols.

evidence.label: Primary - BCVA Change at Week 48
evidence.text: Adjusted mean change in BCVA from baseline at weeks 40, 44, 48: faricimab +6.6 letters vs aflibercept +6.6 letters; estimated treatment difference 0.0 letters (95% CI -1.4 to +1.5). Non-inferiority margin of -4 letters met. Primary endpoint met, replicating TENAYA.

evidence.label: Secondary - Dosing Interval
evidence.text: 78% of LUCERNE faricimab patients achieved Q12W or longer dosing interval by wk 48; 44% achieved Q16W. Central subfield thickness comparable to aflibercept.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind with masked BCVA examiners. D3 LOW - 94% completion. D4 LOW - ETDRS BCVA. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Treatment-naïve choroidal neovascularization (CNV) secondary to age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) in the study eye~* Ability to comply with the study protocol, in the investigator's judgment~* For women of childbearing potential: agreement to remain abstinent (refrain from heterosexual intercourse) or use acceptable contraceptive measures that result in failure rate <1% per year during the treatment period and for at least 3 months after the final dose of study trea...

title: RapidMeta Ophthalmology | Faricimab for Neovascular Age-Related Macular Degeneration v1.1
